commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

commit.message:
fix: use stdin piping for Claude CLI to avoid Windows arg parsing issues

--append-system-prompt with long multiline content breaks argument
parsing on Windows, causing -p to not be recognized. Switch Claude
to the same stdin piping approach used by Codex: combine system
context + user prompt and pipe via stdin with -p flag (no positional
prompt arg).

when a quiz is completed, enabling Kajabi automations. Non-blocking:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Route MITM traffic through Pingora pipeline, add HTTP cache and enhanced threat detection

Major architectural change: MITM-decrypted CONNECT tunnels now flow through
Pingora's ProxyHttp pipeline instead of a custom relay loop, enabling caching,
filtering, and per-request logging for intercepted HTTPS traffic.

Key changes:
- Replace mitm/relay.rs with MitmStream (Pingora IO adapter) + DashMap context passing
- Add HTTP response cache (Pingora MemCache with LRU eviction) with per-origin cache keys
- Add SSRF protection in upstream_peer (pre-connection DNS check)
- Wire server shutdown signal through MITM pipeline
- Add block_reason, rule_name, threat_signals to log entries and UI
- Enhance T0 heuristics: cert risk, security headers, thin TLD coverage, mixed-script detection
- Add T2 thin page detection (low visible text ratio)
- Add brotli/zstd/deflate decompression for content inspection
- Add trusted category protection for reputation cache
- Remove homoglyph brand list in favor of domain-agnostic mixed-script check
- Remove custom HTTP relay loop (704 lines) in favor of Pingora pipeline reuse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Assert when --denomination-asset and --strategy-file are both provided

These flags are incompatible: --strategy-file reads the reserve asset from
the strategy's create_trading_universe(), so --denomination-asset is redundant
and causes the deployment to silently take the wrong code path (legacy
single-chain instead of universe-based multichain).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: A Python framework for managing positions and trades in DeFi

commit.message:
fix(api): strip sslmode from DATABASE_URL to prevent pg verify-full override (#235) (#239)

The previous fix set ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } on the Pool,
but pg-connection-string parses sslmode= from the URL and overrides
the Pool's ssl option. Fix by stripping sslmode= from the URL before
passing it to the Pool, then configuring SSL entirely via the Pool's
ssl option.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix infinite loading on failed profile fetch and share dialog on desktop

AuthContext: Add .catch()/.finally() to getProfile so loading state always
resolves even if Firestore fails — prevents permanent "Loading..." screen.

Share: Replace touch-point mobile detection with UA check so Windows
touchscreen devices don't trigger the native OS share sheet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(orchestration): use in-memory session for orchestrator agent

The orchestrator was immediately completing because it was reusing
persistent session files from previous runs, causing state pollution.

Changes:
- Use SessionManager.create() instead of SessionManager.open(sessionFile)
- Creates fresh in-memory session for each orchestration
- Avoids file system issues during cleanup
- Added debug logging to track tool call count

This is a cleaner solution than deleting session files or managing
directories, as it eliminates the root cause of state pollution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(upload): stop logo flickering & repeated notifications re-upload loop

Root cause: useAutoUploadEffect re-triggered on every formData change
because uploadHandler was recreated whenever contactData (= formData)
changed. Upload completes → updates formData → handler recreated →
effect re-fires → re-upload → repeat = flickering + N notifications.

Fix (2 files):
- useAutoUploadEffect: track uploaded file via ref to prevent re-upload
  of the same File object. Use refs for uploadHandler and onUploadComplete
  so they don't trigger the effect. Dependency array now only: file,
  upload state, purpose, debug.
- usePhotoUploadLogic: use ref for contactData in useMemo so handler
  is NOT recreated on every formData change (contactData is only used
  for file naming metadata, not upload logic).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: migrate WordPress content locally and add code syntax highlighting

- Replaced GitHub API-based content loading with local `import.meta.glob` via `src/services/local.ts`; hooks are now synchronous and require no async fetching
- Added `scripts/migrate.ts` CLI tool to scrape WordPress REST API into Markdown files under `assets/posts/` with full frontmatter (author, SEO, cover image resolutions)
- Migrated all 10 WordPress blog posts and downloaded their images to `public/blog-images/`
- Extended `src/types/blog.ts` FrontMatter schema with `author`, `coverImage`, `coverImageResolutions`, and `seo` fields
- Added `plugins/wordpress-sync.ts` Vite plugin for optional build-time sync (currently disabled)
- Fixed code block language extraction in `scripts/lib/html-to-md.ts`: custom turndown rule reads `language-*` class from WordPress `<pre>` elements and emits fenced blocks with the correct language specifier (e.g., \`\`\`java)
- Added `highlight.js` as a direct dependency and imported `github-dark` theme in `BlogPostPage.tsx` for syntax-colored code blocks
- Added CSS in `BlogPostPage.css` for code block styling: dark background, horizontal scroll, rounded corners, and inline code with muted background
- Updated `tsconfig.node.json` to include `plugins/` and `scripts/` directories for type checking
- Updated `CLAUDE.md` with full architecture documentation for the local content flow and migration tool

feat: SEO blog posts #19 and #20 — typeform-email-to-respondent + typeform-pipedrive-integration

Post #19 — typeform-email-to-respondent:
- Target keyword: "typeform email to respondent"
- ~2,000 words: confirmation vs real reply distinction → the reply gap → why manual replies don't scale → comparison table (confirmation/manual/Zapier template/FormReply) → setup steps → FAQ (5 questions)
- Key angle: Typeform's built-in respondent email is a confirmation, not a conversation; FormReply drafts the personalized reply in 10 seconds

Post #20 — typeform-pipedrive-integration:
- Target keyword: "typeform pipedrive integration"
- ~2,100 words: why Typeform+Pipedrive work together → 3 setup methods (Zapier/Make/webhook+API) → field mapping table → the first-reply gap → parallel stack with FormReply → who this fits → FAQ (5 questions)
- Key angle: Pipedrive captures and routes the deal; FormReply writes the first reply Pipedrive was never built to send

Both added to app/sitemap.ts | Posts: 20 total

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[datadog_dashboard] Add DD-Terraform-Resource header opt-in framework (#3561)

[datadog_dashboard] Add DD-Terraform-Resource header opt-in framework

Add a platform-level mechanism for Terraform resources to annotate their
API requests with a DD-Terraform-Resource header, enabling backends to
identify which resource type originated a request.

The implementation uses an http.RoundTripper that reads a resource name
from the request context and injects the header. Resources opt in by
wrapping their auth context with utils.WithTerraformResource().

datadog_dashboard is the initial adopter — all four CRUD functions now
send the header.

Address PR feedback: extract resource constant and consolidate tests

- Extract "datadog_dashboard" string to a dashboardResourceName constant
- Refactor transport tests into a table-driven test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Phase 2 — extract Aztec-specific code from libclaudebox

config.ts: Remove hardcoded aztec-packages, aztec user, channel maps.
  - Channel maps now populated dynamically from profile loader via
    setChannelMaps()/getChannelBranches()/getChannelProfiles()
  - Add LOG_BASE_URL + buildLogUrl() for configurable log URLs
  - Defaults are generic (repo, admin, main, localhost:3000)

docker.ts: Replace 6x `profileDir !== "barretenberg-audit"` checks
  with DockerConfig.mountReferenceRepo from profile loader.
  - Parameterize CONTAINER_USER/CONTAINER_HOME (was aztec-dev)
  - Parameterize SLACK_WORKSPACE_DOMAIN (was aztecprotocol)
  - Use buildLogUrl() instead of hardcoded ci.aztec-labs.com
  - Use basename(REPO_DIR) for project key mounts (was aztec-packages)

util.ts: Make extractHashFromUrl use LOG_BASE_URL instead of
  hardcoded ci.aztec-labs.com. Derive PROFILE_KEYWORDS from
  discovered profiles instead of hardcoded list.

mcp/base.ts: Remove credential proxy (Redis tunnel, bastion SSH,
  disk transfer) — moved to aztec/cred-proxy.ts. Remove hardcoded
  ci.aztec-labs.com CI dashboard URL. Remove aztec-dev home fallback.
  Remove aztecprotocol.slack.com domain.

server.ts: Import aztec/config.ts first to set env defaults.
  Discover profiles at startup and populate channel maps.
  Add GC guard flag and 6h interval (was daily).

New files:
  - aztec/config.ts — Aztec deployment env defaults
  - aztec/cred-proxy.ts — Redis/bastion/disk-transfer proxy (extracted)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Skip leaf nodes in nav; bust JS cache via script src ?v=4

Leaf nodes (those with no children) are already visible as dim labels
on their parent's page — navigating into them just shows "No items to
display". getNextNode/getPrevNode now only target navigable nodes
(children.length > 0), doing DFS pre-order among those.

Also version-stamps the <script src> so browsers fetch the new JS
rather than serving a stale cached copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(eslint): add no-does-not-throw rule (#7692)

Add an ESLint rule that bans `assert.doesNotThrow()` in test files.
All entries where it was used are fixed and CLAUDE.md is adjusted,
since the comment is not needed anymore.

commit.message:
fix: restore full anti-fabrication test coverage (1459/1459 passing)

Tests were failing because R17-R19 refactoring renamed ANTI-FABRICATION RULES to
ACCURACY RULES and removed explicit discount keywords. Restored:

buildBusinessContext DISCOUNTS rule: added explicit list of prohibited discount
types (military/veteran, senior, courtesy, seasonal, promotional, etc.)

text-conversation ANTI-FABRICATION RULES block: expanded from discounts-only to
full suite including SERVICES (with HVAC unlisted examples), WARRANTIES, SAVINGS
CLAIMS, FREE SERVICES, POST-BOOKING sections — all required by regression tests.

Net result: 1459/1459 tests passing (was 1444), all anti-fabrication regression
guards active in both buildBusinessContext and text-conversation prompts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix security, performance, and classification reliability

- Move NewsAPI key from hardcoded string to .env via python-dotenv;
  add .gitignore to prevent .env from ever being committed
- Cache yfinance downloads in ticker_data dict; reuse for both the
  stage loop and the chart loop (was downloading 2x per ticker)
- Extend history from 90d to 6mo for more reliable Stage Analysis
- Normalize MA slopes by current price so thresholds work across all
  price levels; fix Stage 3 never firing (Stage 1/3 had identical
  conditions — now distinguished by long_slope direction)
- Improve sentiment scoring with negation detection (3-word window
  before each keyword) and expand keyword lists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Split first-event analysis into two slides for maximum impact

SLIDE 14: "Participants whose first event was organizing had 41% higher odds of returning"
- Big title, single chart focus
- Statistics: OR = 1.41 (95% CI: 1.34–1.48), χ² = 197, p < 0.001

SLIDE 15: "Effect consistent across organizations"
- Shows 12/14 orgs above diagonal (organizing advantage)

Code review improvements:
- Added logistic regression (standard method) with profile likelihood CI
- Added 2x2 contingency table with cell counts
- Manual OR verified against logistic regression (both = 1.4092)
- Woolf method CI matches logistic CI (1.34–1.48)
- Bigger chart text for presentation readability

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch from SQLite/Auth.js to localStorage for all progress tracking

Removes server-side dependencies (better-sqlite3, drizzle-orm, @auth/sveltekit)
and moves all progress, XP, achievements, streaks, and auth to client-side
localStorage. This enables deployment on Vercel serverless without a database.

Drift prevention: 115-check conformance suite with count directives and stale prose fixes

Three-layer drift prevention system:
- Layer A: New validators — shapes count (A1), SHACL test count (A2), standards
  document counts (A3), check count post-check in binary (A4)
- Layer B: {@count:KEY} directive expansion in docs renderer for live ontology counts
- Layer C: Fix all stale v3.0-era prose (130/233/304 → 175/325/637) across 8+ files

Also includes SHACL test improvements: test46 (monodromy pipeline), updated
test30/31/44/45 fixtures, 175 NodeShapes in uor-shapes.ttl, shacl.md table update.

Conformance suite: 110 → 115 checks (0 failures).

Fotokwaliteit naar 4K-niveau met efficiënte compressie

Scanner:
- Camera resolutie: 1920x1440 → 3840x2160 (4K)
- Product images: 1200/1600px → 2000/2400px
- WebP kwaliteit: 0.90 → 0.95 (visueel lossless)
- Capture kwaliteit: 0.95 → 0.97

Product Image API (wat klanten zien):
- Resolutie: 800px → 1600px (retina-scherp)
- Format: JPEG 82% → WebP 88% (kleiner + beter)
- Auto WebP/JPEG op basis van browser support
- Nieuwe v2 cache keys (oude cache wordt automatisch vervangen)

Upload API:
- Output: 1600px → 2400px voor meer detail bij zoom

commit.message:
fix(profile): stop infinite retry loop on activity load failure

- Gate IntersectionObserver on loadError state so failed requests don't re-trigger
- Only render sentinel div when eligible (hasMore && \!loadError)
- Show "Failed to load more — tap to retry" button instead of crashing
- Clicking retry clears loadError so observer re-engages naturally
